rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Henard and Szymanski (2001) to justify a core element of its model: that customer product perceptions are critical to new product success. This claim is then built into the paper's hypothesis structure, where customer perceptions mediate the path from salesperson effort to new product sales. Although the citing study does not reuse the cited paper's meta-analytic method, the citation plays a meaningful conceptual framing role rather than a mere bibliography mention.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Henard and Szymanski (2001) as foundational evidence that customer product perceptions drive new product success. It uses that premise to motivate and position its own mediator-based model of salesforce effort, control systems, and launch performance.
